mnemonics: Zero-Sum Games: Your Gain Is My PAIN (Payoff Always Inverts, No cooperation)
keyPoints: Strictly competitive games are zero-sum games where one player's gain is exactly equal to the other player's loss.
The maximin strategy provides security against the opponent's best countermove, ensuring the best worst-case outcome.
In zero-sum games, the maximin value equals the minimax value when a Nash equilibrium exists.
commonMistakes: Assuming all two-player games are strictly competitive - they must be zero-sum.
Confusing maximin (maximize your minimum payoff) with maximize (simply trying to get the highest payoff).
Thinking that mixed strategy equilibria don't exist in strictly competitive games - they often do.
